round_note: Refired on 2026-04-27 against the live tree. The stale premise 'hexa runtime is blocked on this host' no longer holds in the strong form: default bare execution is shim-gated on Darwin, but the documented override HEXA_SHIM_NO_DARWIN_LANDING=1 allows the model-registry hexa witnesses to execute and pass. The strategic verdict still stays TS-primary because runtime adoption remains on the TypeScript side.
trigger: user 2026-04-27 prompt: fire kick(omega-cycle) on 'model-registry.ts port ROI' with stratum=drill, axes=design-strategy + falsifier-coverage, and land witness JSON at the declared path.

alpha.consumed_how: Kept source-of-truth split as the primary failure mode, ruling out any recommendation that promotes peer-edited TS and hexa registries.

beta.consumed_how: Forced the analysis away from a binary port/don't-port frame and into four concrete states: cutover now, keep shadow asset, abandon, or adopt on trigger.

gamma.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_icecube invocation=tool/icecube_neutrino_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)
gamma.consumed_how: Preserved the instrumentation framing: executable witness presence matters, but it is still separate from proof of live runtime adoption.

core_5op_kernel: 3) fan out K>=3 orthogonal axes (raw 48 correlation < 0.7)
5) emit witness JSON to declared landing path

runtime_and_test_adoption_snapshot.hexa_default_exec_result: hexa test_registry/test_parity without override -> rc=76 darwin shim gate
runtime_and_test_adoption_snapshot.hexa_override_exec_result: HEXA_SHIM_NO_DARWIN_LANDING=1 hexa test_registry.hexa -> PASS (35/35); test_resolver.hexa -> PASS (54/54); test_parity.hexa -> PASS (30/30)

callsite_inventory.grep_summary: repo grep found active runtime consumers only on the TS side; the hexa model-registry subtree is exercised by its own tests/CLI/docs but no non-test hexa caller was found.

intake.core_observation: The port already exists and is executable with the documented Darwin override, but the live runtime graph still terminates at the TypeScript ModelRegistry and its TS consumers.

pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: Every Tier-1 must cite at least two concrete units such as LOC, test counts, consumer counts, or blocker counts.
At least 3 distinct strategic states must be enumerated and the rejected ones must be named explicitly.
All path, LOC, grep, and test claims must be host-verifiable from this round.
K>=3 orthogonal axes and pairwise conceptual correlation below 0.7.
Alpha, beta, gamma envelopes must show how they were consumed.
No recommendation may declare the hexa port runtime-authoritative while TS retains the only witnessed live consumers.
A stale assumption corrected by fresh evidence must materially update the reasoning, not be silently ignored.
Emit the exact raw-80 sentinel after witness landing.
As drill stratum, the verdict must be re-firable and trigger-gated rather than terminal.
Every Tier-1 promotion must list its falsifier pass pattern.
Adjacent hexa dependencies must be considered where they affect cutover readiness.
Concrete failure modes must be expressible as reason=<slug> fix=<directive>.

axes.name: cutover-state
axes.finding: Negative for immediate runtime cutover. The TS registry remains the only witnessed live runtime owner, with 10 source consumers and 17 test consumers in coding-agent.

axes.name: shadow-asset-value
axes.finding: Positive for keeping the hexa port as migration capital. The hexa subtree is substantial and verified: 1123 LOC runtime plus 716 LOC of hexa witnesses/CLI support.

axes.name: adoption-triggers
axes.finding: Adoption remains conditional. No non-test hexa consumer, no hexa-side built-in provider database, and auth/request-resolution parity are still incomplete at the runtime boundary.

axes.name: execution-readiness
axes.finding: The earlier stronger blocker has narrowed. Hexa is not dead-on-host; it is default-gated on Darwin and passes when run with the documented override. This improves confidence in the port but does not by itself justify cutover.

candidate_options.name: Promote the hexa port to runtime owner now
candidate_options.reason: Executable witnesses now exist, but no live runtime consumer has moved and the TS side still owns request auth, provider registration lifecycle, and current app integration.

candidate_options.name: Keep TS as runtime source-of-truth and retain hexa as verified shadow/parity asset
candidate_options.reason: Best current return: preserves live runtime stability while keeping the port and its passing hexa witness suite available for future adoption.

candidate_options.name: Treat the hexa subtree as sunk cost and stop maintaining it
candidate_options.reason: Negative ROI because it discards 1123 LOC of runtime port and 716 LOC of executable witness infrastructure that already pass.

candidate_options.name: Keep the port dormant until explicit adoption triggers fire
candidate_options.reason: Valid future path, but no trigger is witnessed in the current tree.

tier_1_promotions.claim: TypeScript remains the runtime owner for now. It has the only witnessed live runtime consumers, 52 passing targeted TS tests, and still carries the active application integration seams.
The hexa model-registry port is no longer just documentary. With HEXA_SHIM_NO_DARWIN_LANDING=1, its registry, resolver, and parity witnesses all pass, so abandoning it would be negative ROI.
Immediate cutover is still rejected. Promotion from shadow asset to runtime owner must wait for a real non-test consumer and the remaining adjacent-runtime parity steps.

falsifier_results.reason: Tier-1 claims cite LOC, consumer counts, and executed test counts.
Four distinct options A-D were enumerated and scored.
All cited paths, counts, and test results were verified by rg, wc, sed, and local command execution in this round.
K=4 and max pairwise conceptual correlation is 0.47.
All three noise envelopes are tagged with consumed_by and consumed_how.
No promotion claims the hexa side is runtime-authoritative while live consumers remain TS-only.
The stale host-exec assumption was corrected by re-running the hexa witnesses with the documented override.
The raw-80 sentinel is recorded in this witness and emitted after landing.
The adoption verdict is explicitly trigger-gated and re-firable.
All Tier-1 promotions include explicit falsifier pass patterns.
Adjacent auth-storage and request-resolution dependencies remain part of the cutover analysis.
Failure modes below are expressed in reason/fix form.

reason_fix_register.fix: promote only after the first real non-test caller depends on core/model-registry rather than its self-tests or CLI harness
re-score adoption after request-time auth/header parity is wired across auth-storage and resolve-config-value boundaries
re-score cutover once the hexa path has a real built-in provider inventory instead of caller-supplied empties
treat bare-host execution as policy-gated; use the documented override or CI landing when validating hexa witnesses

roi_verdict.summary: The live tree now proves two things at once: the hexa model-registry port is real and executable, and immediate cutover is still premature. Current ROI is positive for keeping the hexa subtree as a verified shadow/parity asset, but negative for making it the runtime owner before a real consumer and the remaining adjacent parity work land.

honesty_triad.write_barrier: No product code changed. Only the witness JSON at the declared landing path was refreshed.
no_fabrication_evidence: repo grep over hive/nexus/anima found TS runtime consumers and zero non-test hexa consumers for core/model-registry
npx vitest --run test/model-registry.test.ts executed in /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/coding-agent and passed 52 tests
hexa witnesses re-run locally: test_registry 35/35 PASS, test_resolver 54/54 PASS, test_parity 30/30 PASS, all with HEXA_SHIM_NO_DARWIN_LANDING=1
bare hexa invocation without the override reproduced the Darwin shim gate rc=76

next_round_handoff.purpose: Re-fire only on adoption signal, not on schedule
retirement_triggers_to_watch: A real non-test consumer starts depending on core/model-registry/
hexa-side built-in provider inventory lands and is used by the runtime path
request-time auth/header parity lands across auth-storage and resolve-config-value boundaries
dual-maintenance churn becomes measurable across at least two concrete changesets
next_round_handoff.do_not_fire_unless: one of the listed triggers is witnessed
